title: Scope A T4: wire rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded into fab doctor --fix
description: Call `rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded(projectRoot, { trigger: 'doctor' })` as part of the `fab doctor --fix` pass. Idempotent: re-running --fix on a freshly-rotated ledger is a no-op. Surface rotation as a 'fixed' entry only when it actually rotated (archivedCount > 0).

files.target: doctor --fix pipeline (near line 1080, after event_ledger_partial_write block)
files.change: Add new `--fix` step that calls `await rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded(projectRoot)`. When `rotated === true`, push a synthetic `fixed` entry describing the rotation (count + archive_path) into the fix report. Idempotent — no entry when archivedCount === 0.

Add tests: (1) `fab doctor --fix` on ledger with old lines triggers rotation + reports archived count; (2) re-running --fix on freshly-rotated ledger is no-op (no second rotation, no duplicate fixed entry); (3) --fix on ledger with no old lines does NOT create an empty archive file; (4) events_rotated audit event appears in resulting main ledger after first --fix; (5) same-day re-rotation appends to existing archive (regression for T3 contract).

implementation: Add fix step inside the doctor.ts fix pipeline — place after counter_desync/reconcile blocks so meta is stable before rotation
Build 'fixed' synthetic entry only when archivedCount > 0 (preserves idempotency contract)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test doctor` to confirm green

reference.examples: truncateLedgerToLastNewline integration is the closest peer — mirror the call shape + fixed-entry push

criteria: `fab doctor --fix` triggers rotation when retention-window-eligible lines exist
Idempotent: second --fix run on the same state produces zero additional rotation work
events_rotated audit event present in main ledger after --fix with non-zero archive
Same-day re-fix appends to existing archive file (no overwrite)
5 new doctor.test.ts tests pass; existing doctor tests unaffected

rationale.chosen_approach: Primary trigger via doctor --fix (fits 三入口 from feedback_cli_design); no background cron, no separate ledger subcommand
alternatives_considered: Background cron in MCP server (cross-process race risk)
Dedicated `fab ledger rotate` subcommand (违反三入口)
rationale.tradeoffs: Users must remember to run `fab doctor --fix` periodically (mitigated by 50MB soft-warn from T3)

manual_checks: Run `fab doctor --fix` on a manually-aged events.jsonl, inspect events.archive/
Re-run --fix immediately; confirm no second rotation
